Tribute show

A TRIBUTE TO LATE-NIGHT RADIO · 1978–1992

Midnight Tape Orchestra

with The Cassette Choir & Halftone Quartet opening the night

NOV
14
Thursday, Nov 14
Doors 7:00 PM · Show 8:15 PM
Atlas Atrium
Birchwood District, Portland
All ages welcome
Step-free entry
2h with intermission
Moderate volume
Get tickets · from $32
Add to itinerary

STEP 1 · CHOOSE YOUR SEATS

Ticket tiers
Prices include venue fees · taxes calculated at checkout
General admission
Floor
Stand or move freely on the main floor. Best if you like being close to the stage and the energy of the crowd.
from$32
per person · all-in
~210 of 340 remaining
Select
Seated balcony
Reserved seat
An assigned upholstered seat on the upper tier with a wide view of the stage. Quieter and ideal for first-time concertgoers.
from$54
per person · row C–H
Only 41 of 148 left
Select
Early-entry pass
30-min head start
Enter the venue at 6:30 PM with a smaller group, settle in calmly, pick your spot, and grab a drink before doors open to the general crowd.
from$72
includes GA floor
Only 9 of 80 left — almost gone
Select

BEGINNER ORIENTATION

What to expect
Show length
Approximately 2 hours including a 20-minute intermission — you'll be out by around 10:30 PM with time to catch the last train.
2h total
Volume level
Comfortable, conversational between songs. Loud during the brass-heavy second set, but never punishing — basic earplugs are stocked at the bar if you want them.
2 / 5
Standing vs seated
The floor is standing-only with a few rail spots; the balcony is fully seated. If standing for 2 hours feels long, the balcony tier is the safer pick.
Mixed
Crowd vibe
Warm, attentive, slightly older audience that mostly comes for the songs — expect polite applause, swaying, and a little singing along on the encore rather than a mosh.
Gentle
FIRST SHOW? READ THIS.

A two-minute primer on doors, drinks, and the small things nobody tells newcomers.

Arrive 20 minutes before doors
Bring photo ID and the QR ticket
Coats can be checked for $3
Small bags only — no backpacks
Earplugs available, free at the bar
Read the full guide
